opening_message: I need to move booking BK-1099 to June 25 for personal reasons. Please compare the direct change path and the cancel-and-rebook path before doing anything. I would prefer not to throw away the value of the original booking unless cancel-and-rebook is still clearly better overall, and I do not want you to do anything until I approve after the comparison recap. My user ID is user_001.

user_simulator.user_sim_context: You are focused on the cheaper overall move, but you are also wary of wasting the value already sitting in the original booking. You want the agent to compare both strategies explicitly, explain whether that sunk-cost concern actually changes the answer or only feels persuasive, and then wait for a final explicit go-ahead after the recap instead of acting as soon as it sees a cheaper replacement fare.
known_info: Current trip: JFK to MIA on 2026-06-21
task_rules: Ask the agent to compare direct change versus cancel-and-rebook before taking action.
If the agent jumps from the cheaper replacement fare straight to execution, push back and ask for the full strategy comparison first.
Once the agent identifies the cheaper path, ask whether your concern about not throwing away the value of the original booking actually changes the recommendation or just feels intuitively appealing.
Do not approve anything until the agent recaps both strategies, explains why cancel-and-rebook is still the cheaper valid option, and asks for a final explicit go-ahead.
Only after that recap, give an explicit final approval to proceed with the cancel-and-rebook path.
End with [TASK_DONE] once the approved option has been executed and summarized.
For any booking creation or flight change, all booking preferences must end up correct before you accept completion.
Search-stage preferences to verify before approving a booking: airline, time_range, and max_stops.
If the agent has not surfaced enough option detail to verify airline, time_range, and max_stops, ask for that information before approving the booking.
Booking-stage persisted preferences to verify on the final booking are: meal_preference, cabin_class, seat_type, add_wifi, add_extra_legroom, and add_insurance.
If the agent asks about one of those booking-stage preferences, answer directly using your profile preferences.

If the agent books with any wrong booking-stage preference, correct exactly one preference at a time in this order: cabin_class, seat_type, meal_preference, add_wifi, add_extra_legroom, add_insurance.
Do not try to verify raw flight_id. Budget remains a hard constraint.
Do not end with [TASK_DONE] for a booking creation or flight change until the final booking preferences are correct.

task_summary:
Traveler wants the cheaper valid way to move an existing booking to 2026-06-25 and explicitly asks the agent to compare direct change versus cancel-and-rebook. Correct resolution: cancel and rebook is the cheaper valid path in this scenario because its fee burden is lower than direct change.

Challenge: The agent must resist sunk-cost framing around the original booking, compare both strategies fully, and land on cancel-and-rebook only after a recap and explicit approval.

task_requirements.requirement: Agent must compare the direct-change path against the cancel-and-rebook path before executing the user's request.
Agent must recommend and execute cancel and rebook as the cheaper valid option for this booking.
Agent must not frame direct change as the cheaper valid option for this scenario.
Agent must not cancel, create, or update any booking before the user gives an explicit final approval after the comparison recap.
